utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 6 items required in the final format: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. All 6 items appear in the final property solution. Now check if each is fully specified: 1) property: includes selected property with details and price (address, bedrooms, bathrooms, square_feet, price, school_district, neighborhood, property_taxes, status) — fully specified. 2) financing: includes financing solution with terms (lender, mortgage_amount, down_payment, interest_rate, monthly_payment, amortization, special_program, approval_status) — fully specified. 3) legal_services: legal services with costs (lawyer, cost, contact, status) — fully specified. 4) moving_services: moving arrangements with costs (company, contact, cost, date, route, services, status) — fully specified. 5) setup_services: utility and setup services (utility_package, cost, includes, status) — fully specified. 6) total_budget: complete cost breakdown (property_price, down_payment, closing_costs, legal_services, property_inspection, moving_services, utility_setup, total_upfront_cost, monthly_mortgage_payment, remaining_equity, grand_total_package) — fully specified. Therefore, all 6/6 items are covered.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final property solution includes selections across multiple categories. We must match each item to the exact name in the ground-truth ratings and provide the corresponding numeric rating. Mappings found: Property choice corresponds to a purchase property; however, the provided address does not appear in the ground-truth list, so no direct property rating can be mapped. Financing uses a Traditional Mortgage (purchase_financing). Legal services match Basic Legal Package. Moving services align most closely with Standard Moving (professional service at a moderate price). Utility setup explicitly matches Premium Utilities. Other listed budget items like inspection are not named to a specific survey type, so we avoid mapping them.
